title: Desktop measurement opt-in feature flag (default OFF)

files_forbidden: any component, route handler, message catalog, or scanner module

acceptance_criteria: A new boolean flag (e.g. desktopPerformance) is added to the frozen `features` object following the existing 15-flag pattern, DEFAULTING TO false, overridable via the existing FEATURES env JSON.
The flag is observable via the existing getFeatureStatus()/feature-status surface exactly like the other flags (no bespoke mechanism).
isFeatureReady (or the established readiness pattern) treats the flag correctly: when false, no desktop behaviour is configured/enabled.

When the flag is false the documented contract is that downstream desktop behaviour is byte-identical to today's mobile-only path (this task only defines the flag; the byte-identical guarantee is verified where the flag is consumed in T-06).
.env.example documents the new flag under the existing FEATURES/optional-features section with a one-line description (free quota cost tradeoff noted).
Existing features.ts tests still pass; a test asserts the new flag defaults to false and flips via FEATURES JSON. npm run lint and npm run test pass for touched code.
output_format: code plus a plain-English note on why desktop defaults OFF (quota) and how the flag is read
